title: Cleistes paranaensis
taxonomy: Cleistes paranaensis is classified within the family Orchidaceae, specifically within the subfamily Vanilloideae. This orchid species is further categorized under the tribe Vanilleae. The genus Cleistes, to which Cleistes paranaensis belongs, is a group of terrestrial orchids native to South America. Cleistes paranaensis is native to the subtropical regions of southern Brazil and northern Argentina. The classification of Cleistes paranaensis highlights its unique position within the diverse and complex family of orchids.

intro: Cleistes paranaensis, commonly known as the Parana Cleistes, is a species of orchid found in the subtropical regions of South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, inflammation, and fever, due to its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Cleistes paranaensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Cleistes paranaensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and coughs.
